title: 7 Steps to Build a Project Initiation Document (PID)
summary: A concise guide that outlines the seven essential steps for creating a complete Project Initiation Document, covering context, scope, work breakdown, resourcing, responsibilities, risk management, and stakeholder distribution.
content: Creating an effective PID involves seven sequential steps: 1) Provide the Context, 2) Define the Project Parameters, 3) Define the Specifics, 4) Define the Project Breakdown Structure and Resourcing Plan, 5) Define Who’s Who (RACI), 6) Identify Risks, Assumptions, Issues and Dependencies, and 7) Share the PID with the team and stakeholders. Following this structure ensures comprehensive coverage of all critical project information.
can_answer: What are the seven steps to create a Project Initiation Document?
How should I structure a PID?
